title: Living Doc Compositor Development Overview
subtitle: A working view of the tool as runtime, schema language, template system, and AI-ready reasoning substrate.
scope: Development of the living-doc compositor across runtime UX, renderer behavior, convergence language, templates, and operational tooling.
sourceCoverage: Repo-local code, concept docs, template files, registry state, recent git history, and current GitHub issues inspected through gh CLI on 2026-04-15.
objective: Make the tool legible as one evolving system instead of a pile of UI work, renderer changes, schema decisions, and concept notes.
successCondition: A contributor can see the active development lanes, the model constraints that must not drift, the tools used to evolve the repo, and the ground-truth operating decisions that shape new work.

callouts.title: How to use this doc
items: Use the capability and tooling sections to orient current work before opening individual files.
Use the formal-model and decision sections to avoid weakening the schema and snapshot contract while the tool grows quickly.

callout.title: Current pressure pattern
items: Growth is happening in two directions at once: wider product/runtime surface and deeper formal semantics.
The active issue set clusters around left-rail UX, prompt-driven skill creation, snapshot lineage, renderer quality, and stronger convergence typing.

data.name: Embedded compositor runtime and editor surface
notes: The compositor is now the full runtime that must ship inside every rendered HTML, not a reduced viewer.
Current pressure is in the two-layer rail and drawer architecture plus the prompt sidecar that turns the current living doc into agent-ready context.

data.name: Registry and convergence-language evolution
notes: The type language is expanding as migrated and newly created docs reveal stronger recurring semantic patterns.
The critical quality bar is that convergence types remain inference-time contracts instead of becoming loose hints patched by local presentation overrides.

data.name: Renderer quality and portable snapshot output
notes: The renderer must preserve source fidelity while still producing standalone HTML that feels intentional enough to share.
Identity and lineage are now part of the output contract, but the visual-density and content-fit work is still an active lane.

data.name: Templates and reusable skill-generation surface
notes: Templates are no longer just starter files; they are becoming a stronger semantic harness for reusable workflows and agent prompts.
The prompt layer is moving away from selector-heavy packaging and toward document-derived skill identity built from registry, payload, and template semantics.

data.name: Convergence type defines semantic and structural contract
data.definition: A convergence type is a semantic claim about what kind of convergence is happening, and it should determine the expected structural shape.
data.authorityScope: Registry definitions plus renderer and compositor behavior for all living docs.
violations: Section-level projection and column overrides can still blur type identity when they redefine structure instead of only presenting data.
notes: This matters because the repo is building living docs as an AI reasoning substrate, not just a page renderer.

data.name: Canonical JSON and portable HTML stay distinct
data.definition: Canonical JSON owns the editable document state; rendered HTML is the portable artifact used for sharing and local reasoning.
data.authorityScope: Rendering, sharing, and rerender workflows inside this repo and across sibling repos.
notes: Visible identity and lineage metadata exist to make drift legible rather than pretending portability does not create forks.

data.name: The registry is the single source of truth for type language
data.definition: New convergence types belong in the registry instead of being smuggled into renderer branches or compositor-only logic.
data.authorityScope: All templates, docs, renderer behavior, and compositor affordances in this repo.
notes: This is the contract that keeps future doc creation data-driven instead of ad hoc.

data.name: Template semantics shape workflow identity without overriding schema
data.definition: Template semantics can shape trigger conditions, workflow order, and skill identity while the registry slice and current payload remain authoritative for the specific document instance.
data.authorityScope: Prompt generation and reusable skill creation for template-backed living docs.
notes: This is the deeper semantic move that turns templates into workflow harnesses instead of just sample files.

data.name: Universal renderer
data.purpose: Render canonical JSON into a standalone HTML snapshot with the full compositor embedded.
data.whenToUse: Use whenever a living doc needs to be refreshed, shared, or moved into another reasoning context.
notes: This is the main bridge between editable JSON and portable artifact.
caveats: Rendered HTML can drift from canonical JSON, so rerender discipline matters.

data.name: Embedded compositor sync
data.purpose: Keep the checked-in rendered docs aligned with the current compositor runtime embedded inside them.
data.whenToUse: Use after editing `docs/living-doc-compositor.html` and before assuming the shipped examples match current behavior.
notes: This catches drift inside the repo's own examples before it leaks into external rerenders.
caveats: Syncing local examples does not refresh cross-repo living docs; those still need explicit rerenders from their source JSON.

data.name: Registry overview generator
data.purpose: Expose the current convergence-language surface so contributors can decide whether new work needs a new type or just a new document instance.
data.whenToUse: Use when repo changes start feeling like hidden schema work rather than just GUI or renderer work.
caveats: The overview reflects the registry as written; it does not validate whether sibling repos are already drifting from that language.

data.name: Repo agent instructions
data.purpose: Keep contributors and code agents aligned on rendering rules, registry authority, and cross-repo living-doc discovery.
data.whenToUse: Use before GUI changes, rerenders, or cross-repo living-doc maintenance work.
data.invocation: Open `AGENTS.md` before changing compositor behavior or rerendering docs outside this repo.
notes: The current instructions also point agents at the external living-doc catalog when the source document lives in another repo.
caveats: The catalog itself lives outside this repo in `~/.gtd/living-docs.json`, so local repo links cannot fully encode that discovery layer.

data.name: Rendered living docs must embed the complete compositor
notes: The shared artifact is the document plus the tool, not a viewer that strips authoring power.
Any rerendered HTML should carry the complete runtime even when opened from another repo or shared as a snapshot.

data.name: Type knowledge belongs in the registry, not in per-doc special cases
notes: When a recurring pattern appears, the answer is to promote it into an explicit convergence type rather than letting local rendering mutations carry semantic meaning.
This keeps the type system strong enough for both human and model reasoning.

data.name: Prompt UI should stay simple while prompt logic gets smarter
notes: The prompt panel should expose one useful prompt rather than a selector matrix.
Higher-order behavior belongs in prompt-shaping logic derived from registry, payload, template semantics, and repo references.

data.name: Cross-repo living-doc discovery is catalog work, not registry work
notes: The registry defines the living-doc language, but the actual cross-repo document inventory is maintained in `~/.gtd/living-docs.json`.
That separation matters because document location and sync workflow are operational metadata, not convergence-type schema.
